notes: Country-level VIZA RAG seed for norway. This file is the source of truth for this country's visitor/tourism visa knowledge and is designed to evolve alongside the country-specific form-filling workflow.

documents.title: Norway Visitor Visa and Schengen Short Stay

chunks.title: 中国护照挪威短期旅游签证建议
chunks.content: 中国护照持有人计划去挪威短期旅游，且挪威是唯一目的地或主要停留国家时，VIZA AI 应推荐 Norway/Schengen visitor visa 路线。先确认国籍、合法居住地、挪威是否为停留最长或主目的地、是否同时去冰岛/法国/意大利/瑞士等其他申根国家、计划停留天数和入境次数需求。不要把挪威问题路由到美国 B1/B2、英国 Standard Visitor、加拿大 TRV 或东南亚签证。

chunks.title: Norway visitor visa requirements and funds
chunks.content: UDI states that a visitor visa can be used for a visit to Norway/Schengen of up to 90 days. Core checks include paying the application fee, submitting the embassy checklist, holding a passport valid for at least 3 months after the visit and issued within the last 10 years, valid travel insurance, sufficient money for the stay and return journey, no entry ban, Schengen 90/180 compliance, and fingerprint registration. UDI gives a funding benchmark of at least NOK 500 per day in Norway/Schengen, or a financial guarantee from the person/company visited if the applicant cannot show enough funds.

chunks.title: Norway application channel in China
chunks.content: Norway's China service pages direct short-stay visa applicants to register the application online with UDI/Application Portal and submit documents through VFS Global in China. VIZA AI should ask the applicant's legal residence city/country before giving appointment guidance because the visa application centre and checklist can differ by jurisdiction. Remind users that VFS is a service provider and may charge service fees in addition to the visa fee.

documents.title: Norway Application Form and Document Intake Requirements

chunks.title: Norway application channel and form scope
chunks.content: Use the competent Norway consulate or its authorised visa application centre when Norway is the only destination, main destination, longest-stay destination, or first-entry state when stays are equal. Also check the country-specific source: https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/visit-and-holiday/visitors-visa-to-norway/?c=xxk. Official source URLs reviewed for this seed: https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/document/download/5fe79baa-2d46-4b65-84ec-ea2e9b5ab1d6_en?filename=Visa+Application+Form.pdf ; https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/visit-and-holiday/visitors-visa-to-norway/?c=xxk. This RAG chunk is for visitor/tourism-oriented intake and form-filling preparation; route work, study, residence, immigration, journalism or other regulated purposes to a separate country workflow.

chunks.title: Norway fields to collect before filling the form
chunks.content: The harmonised Schengen form intake should collect surname/current and birth names, first names, date/place/country of birth, current and birth nationality, sex, civil status, parental authority for minors, national ID where applicable, travel document type/number/issue/expiry/issuing authority, home address/email/phone, residence in another country if any, occupation, employer or school, travel purpose, destination Member State(s), first-entry Member State, number of entries requested, intended arrival/departure dates and duration, prior Schengen fingerprints, entry permit for final destination if any, inviting person/company or hotel/accommodation, who pays travel/living costs, EU/EEA/Swiss family member data if applicable, and applicant declaration/signature.

chunks.title: Norway supporting documents and review checklist
chunks.content: Core Schengen evidence includes signed visa application form, passport/travel document valid beyond the planned Schengen exit, photo, travel medical insurance, proof of purpose, itinerary/transport, accommodation or host invitation, evidence of financial means, evidence of intention to return, residence/jurisdiction proof, fee, and biometrics where required. Workflow guardrails: VIZA should determine the competent Schengen state before filling forms. For multi-country trips, compute longest stay or first entry if equal; collect day split and accommodation per country. Do not submit or sign declarations without explicit user confirmation.

chunks.title: Standard passport identity-field answer rules
chunks.content: Standard-answer identity fields must be answered from the applicant's passport biodata page, MRZ, official identity document, or the official dropdown options. This applies to passport number, name, date of birth, sex, nationality, issuing country, issue date, expiry date, issuing authority, place of issue, and passport/document type. For issuing authority or place-of-issue questions, do not infer the answer from the pickup city, residence city, application country, or travel destination; copy the exact Authority / Issuing authority / Place of issue wording printed on the passport if present. For Chinese ordinary passports, newer valid passports may show National Immigration Administration, PRC / 中华人民共和国国家移民管理局; older valid passports may show MPS Exit & Entry Administration / 公安部出入境管理局. If the passport prints a different authority, use the passport text. For passport type, select Ordinary / Regular / Normal only for ordinary personal passports; select Diplomatic, Official, Service, Special, Travel document, Refugee, or Other only when the document explicitly indicates that type. Official sources reviewed for this standard field rule include China National Immigration Administration certificate-issuing-authority notices and the Passport Law of the People's Republic of China; the passport biodata page remains the final source for the applicant's individual answer.

chunks.title: Official-source field answer norms
chunks.content:
Official-source field answer norms for norway.
Use these norms only as filling guidance; if a live official form, passport, or uploaded document shows a more specific value, that document controls the answer.

Crawled source URLs: https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/visit-and-holiday/visitors-visa-to-norway/?c=xxk ; https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/document/download/5fe79baa-2d46-4b65-84ec-ea2e9b5ab1d6_en?filename=Visa+Application+Form.pdf

Passport and identity fields:
- You must have a passport that is valid for at least three months after the end of your visit.
- The passport must be issued within the last ten years.
- If you, for example, have a diplomatic, service or special passport, special travel documents or a residence permit in a Schengen country, you may not need a visitor visa.

Issue, expiry, and validity fields:
- This visa is valid for entry to all Schengen countries. 
- You must have a passport that is valid for at least three months after the end of your visit.

Travel plan and port fields:
- This visa is valid for entry to all Schengen countries. 
- You must not be subject to a prohibition against entry to Norway or Schengen .

Accommodation, host, and address fields:
- NOK 300 per day is regarded as a sufficent minimum if you will be staying with family or friends, or in accommodation that has been paid for in advance.
- NOK 1,300 per day is regarded as sufficient if you have not documented pre-booked or pre-paid accommodation for the entire duration of your stay.
- Remember to have the four-word sponsor code ready when you apply for a visitor visa (if your sponsor uses the electronic form).

Purpose, occupation, work, and study fields:
- If you are going to Norway on business, you will normally be granted a visa.
- If the meeting you will be attending is important to your business or the business you will be visiting, you have a better chance of being granted a visa.
- You cannot work , including remote work , while you are visiting Norway on a visitor visa.

Funds, insurance, photo, and supporting-document fields:
- Whether your funds are considered sufficient will be assessed individually in each case.
- If you do not have enough money yourself, you must have a financial guarantee: The person or company you are visiting must fill out a sponsorship form, which can be either electronic or on a paper form .

Answering rule: for closed-list fields, prefer the exact official option label/value. For document-backed fields, copy the passport, official identity document, application account, itinerary, invitation, accommodation, insurance, funds, or uploaded support document rather than guessing from a city, country, or prior answer.
